subject: Project Management
title: People Risk
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on People Risk.

hook.text: Your best developer gets a better offer and leaves next Friday. Now what?

buildup.text: People risk includes turnover, illness, conflicts, and key-person dependency — the 'bus factor.'

discovery.text: If only one person understands a critical system, the project is one resignation away from crisis.

twist.text: People risk is the hardest to manage because it involves humans — unpredictable, emotional, and irreplaceable.

climax.text: Cross-training, documentation, and team redundancy are the defenses — none are glamorous, all are essential.

punchline.text: Your project's biggest risk has a name and a LinkedIn profile.

quiz.question: What is the 'bus factor' in project management?
options: How many people can leave before the project is in crisis
A method for estimating bus routes
